findings.description: dbt manifest.json embeds absolute host paths via root_path in every node. partial_parse.msgpack caches these. When manifest is built on host and COPYed into Docker, dbt operations fail with path mismatch.
findings.proposedRule: P67: Delete partial_parse.msgpack and regenerate manifests inside Docker container after COPY

findings.source: PR #213 review comment by greptile-apps[bot] on helm-ci.yaml
findings.description: azure/setup-helm@v4 is the GitHub Action version, not the Helm binary version. The version: input parameter controls the actual binary. Confusing the two caused decision D4 to be wrong (believed CI ran Helm v4 when it ran v3.14.0).
findings.proposedRule: P68: Always distinguish GitHub Action version (@vN tag) from tool binary version (version: input)

findings.source: Helm v4 migration research + E2E test failure
findings.description: Helm v3 --atomic implied --wait. Helm v4 renamed it to --rollback-on-failure but dropped the implicit --wait. Without --wait, Helm returns immediately and rollback-on-failure may not detect startup failures.
findings.proposedRule: P69: Always pair --rollback-on-failure with --wait in Helm v4

build.notes: 1I: pre-existing ruff errors in plugin.py (992 violations, all pre-existing)

tests.notes: Unit tests passed. Integration tests require Kind cluster (expected skip on local).

security.notes: 1W was cryptography CVE — resolved by upgrading to 46.0.6. 3I were pre-existing vuln-ignore entries.

wiring.notes: 1W was stale docstring in generate_entry_point_code() — fixed.
